title: Can Opendoor (OPEN) Turn Widening Losses Into a Stronger Unit-Economics Story?
text: Can Opendoor (OPEN) Turn Widening Losses Into a Stronger Unit-Economics Story? The key question for Opendoor investors is whether the company can translate operational improvements into a fundamentally stronger unit-economics story. Recent earnings releases provide mixed signals: Positive indicators include achievement of adjusted EBITDA profitability on a 12-month go-forward basis, Dramatic improvement in newer cohort metrics and resale velocity, Significant AI-driven efficiency gains in assessment and closing processes. Concerning signals include continued GAAP losses at the company level, Year-over-year revenue declines in Q1 2026, Shareholder dilution from recent capital raises and warrant distributions. The resolution of this fundamental question will likely drive stock performance over the next 2-3 years, as investors assess whether the company can sustain operational improvements and achieve adjusted net income profitability by year-end 2026.
